commands.review_summary:
This PR projects nullable and literal-union runtime tool schemas into provider-friendly JSON schema, preserves raw nullable validation for cron/plain JSON unions, isolates cron schema instances, and refreshes affected Codex prompt snapshots.

PR surface: Source +107, Tests +76. Total +183 across 13 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection shows current main passes raw runtime schemas with nullable/const/union composition through the Codex dynamic-tool projection path, while the PR head projects those shapes and adds focused regression tests for cron clear sentinels and union coercion.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainer-authored protected PR changes provider/model-visible schema contracts, so it should stay open for human compatibility approval and current-head mergeability/CI rather than automated repair.

target.title: fix(agents): project nullable tool schemas for providers
target.body:
## Summary

- Project nullable and literal-union tool schemas into the OpenAPI-friendly provider schema shape used for runtime tools.
- Preserve runtime validation semantics for explicit `null` clear sentinels before provider projection.
- Keep plain JSON union coercion ordered, so a numeric branch still wins before a later broad string branch while explicit nullable unions keep `null` intact.
- Make cron clear-sentinel fields real TypeBox unions, then project them to provider-compatible plain `string` / `array` / `object` schema shapes for model-facing output.
- Keep cron tool schema instances isolated so provider projection/normalization cannot mutate the raw schema used by validation or later tool instances.
- Refresh only the prompt snapshots still affected after rebasing onto current `origin/main`.

## Verification

- Latest head: `a23ee0b7012` after rebasing onto current `origin/main` and fixing the autoreview union-coercion finding.
- GitHub CI for `a23ee0b7012` is queued after the force-with-lease push.
- Previous GitHub CI on `ef6bc22dfec` passed: 132 checks passed, 31 skipped, 0 pending, 0 failing.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/llm-core/src/validation.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed 1 shard / 4 tests.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/llm-core/src/validation.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.schema.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts src/cron/service.jobs.test.ts src/cron/service/store.test.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed 4 shards / 221 tests.
- `node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check` passed, `Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 ...` passed on changed TS files.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.json ...` passed on changed TS files.
- `OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions` passed, confirming the stale-base extension lint failure is fixed by current `origin/main` rather than this PR.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- final diff private reported-name scrub passed.
- Autoreview clean after the union-coercion fix: no accepted/actionable findings, `overall: patch is correct (0.84)`.
- Earlier AWS Crabbox changed gate before the latest rebase: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_cbb1c631efeb`, run `run_4415ced3845b`, command `pnpm check:changed`, lanes `core`, `coreTests`, exit 0.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: strict provider adapters should not receive `anyOf`/`oneOf`/`const`/null-type composition from runtime tool schemas when a provider-safe projection is available; runtime validation should still preserve explicit `null` clear sentinels; plain JSON union coercion should preserve branch-order coercion; provider projection must not poison shared cron schema objects.

Real environment tested: local Codex worktree rebased onto current `origin/main`, final head `a23ee0b7012`; previous GitHub CI was green on `ef6bc22dfec`; new GitHub CI is queued for `a23ee0b7012`; earlier AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate covered the provider-projection slice.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: rebased onto current `origin/main`; fixed the raw cron schema to use real nullable TypeBox unions; fixed raw-schema diagnostics before provider projection; fixed union coercion to preserve explicit `null` without bypassing ordered non-null coercion; regenerated prompt snapshots; reran focused cron/provider projection tests, the CI-failing cron/MCP tests, prompt snapshot check, formatter, targeted lint, extension lint, diff check, private-name scrub, and autoreview.

Evidence after fix: focused provider/cron proof passed 4 shards with 221 tests; prompt snapshots are current; local lint/format checks passed; extension oxlint passes on the rebased head; autoreview is clean; earlier remote `pnpm check:changed` completed successfully in run `run_4415ced3845b`.

Observed result after fix: raw cron tool validation accepts `agentId`, `sessionKey`, and `payload.toolsAllow` as `null` clear sentinels, plain JSON union validation preserves numeric coercion before later broad branches, projected cron schemas expose provider-compatible plain `string`/`array`/`object` shapes, and each cron tool instance receives independent nested schema objects.

What was not tested: live Telegram or full assistant-turn E2E; this PR is limited to runtime schema projection, argument validation, cron schema-instance isolation, and prompt snapshot contracts.

## Current CI note

The two failing checks on `c6aaf8003c7` were stale-base extension oxlint failures from base `6cb06f5fbcf5`; current `origin/main` contains `b2a1c5caa85 test(matrix): keep async monitor callbacks lint-clean`. This branch is now rebased on that fixed main and local extension oxlint passes. GitHub CI is queued on `a23ee0b7012`.
target.author: vincentkoc
